description: Voting and promotion rules for the No-Man causal knowledge base. Immutable except by project owner with changelog.md entry.
_changelog_note: 2026-05-30: Added confidence_tier_schema (5-tier epistemic quality system) and audit_fields schema. All existing tested edges migrate to tier 4 (human_verified); speculative edges to tier 1. Audit fields (proposed_at, basis, verified_by, verified_at) are optional and nullable. No promotion rules changed.

confidence_tier_schema.description: Five-tier numeric system for edge epistemic quality. Replaces the binary tested/speculative confidence classification. Chain confidence is computed as the minimum tier of any edge in the chain.

1.name: speculative
1.description: Edge proposed without supporting literature or data. Corresponds to the old 'speculative' status with no citation.

2.name: cross_national_analogy
2.description: Edge supported by evidence from other countries or institutional contexts (e.g., US, European banking studies) but not directly validated for Japan.

3.name: literature_applied_japan
3.description: Edge supported by academic literature directly applicable to Japan (BoJ, RIETI, Japanese banking studies) but not yet independently empirically verified.

4.name: human_verified
4.description: Edge reviewed and confirmed by a domain expert with citation. Conservative default for all edges that passed the tested promotion process. Corresponds to the old 'tested' status.

5.name: empirically_validated
5.description: Edge backed by quantitative empirical test (Granger causality, regression, panel data) confirming the predicted sign with statistical significance in a Japan-relevant dataset.

confidence_tier_schema.chain_rule: The confidence_tier of a chain is the minimum confidence_tier of any edge in the chain. No averaging. No majority rule.

audit_fields_schema.description: Optional audit log fields on every edge. All fields are nullable. Existing edges without these fields remain valid.

proposed_by.description: ID or name of the LLM worker or human who proposed this edge. Already required for speculative edges by invariant 7.

proposed_at.description: Timestamp when this edge was first proposed.

basis.description: Basis for the edge proposal. Use paper DOI or URL when citing a specific paper.

verified_by.description: Name or ID of the human who verified this edge. Null if not yet verified.

verified_at.description: Timestamp when this edge was verified. Null if not yet verified.

proposed_by_values.description: Exhaustive list of valid values for the proposed_by field on any edge in speculative_graph.json or seed_graph.json.

descriptions.literature: Edge proposed based on review of academic literature during graph construction.
descriptions.worker: Edge proposed by a human worker or domain expert through the Socratic dialogue interface.
descriptions.semantic_scholar_candidate: Edge candidate identified by the automated Semantic Scholar citation search (src/literature.py). Requires human review and citation attachment before promotion; the search result alone does not satisfy governance.json §speculative_to_tested.

automated_citation_lookup.description: Rules governing the automated literature search module src/literature.py and its interaction with the governance process.
automated_citation_lookup.purpose: Search Semantic Scholar for papers supporting or documenting speculative causal edges. Results are candidates for human review; they do not automatically satisfy the citation requirement for edge promotion.
what_this_does_not_do: Automatically promote any edge from speculative to tested
Substitute for human review of a citation
Guarantee the sign or direction of a causal relationship
automated_citation_lookup.governance_boundary: A semantic_scholar_candidate edge enters speculative_graph.json only after a human reviewer has read the candidate paper and confirmed it supports the edge sign. The automated search output alone does not satisfy requirement_1_academic_citation.

proposed_to_speculative.description: An edge may be promoted from proposed to speculative if and only if the requirement below is satisfied. This transition is enforced automatically by feedback.py.

adversarial_result_present.description: The edge must have an adversarial_result field populated by run_adversarial_review(). This ensures every proposed edge has been stress-tested by a signed counterclaim before it enters the human review queue.
adversarial_result_present.note: The adversarial review does not need to find a contradicting paper. search_exhausted: true is a valid result. The requirement is that the review was attempted.

speculative_to_tested.description: An edge may be promoted from speculative to tested if and only if both requirements below are satisfied.

requirement_1_academic_citation.description: At least one academic citation must be attached to the edge.
requirement_1_academic_citation.note: Human opinion alone, regardless of seniority, does not satisfy this requirement. Worker votes without citations do not satisfy this requirement.

requirement_2_empirical_validation.description: At least one of the following empirical validation criteria must be met.

minimum_one_of.description: A quantitative test result is attached showing the predicted sign. Accepted methods: Granger causality test, correlation analysis, regression analysis, panel data estimation.
A second academic citation, independent of the first, confirms the same sign for the causal relationship.

speculative_to_rejected.description: A speculative edge is rejected if at least one of the following criteria is met.

minimum_one_of.description: A worker provides a specific counter-argument with supporting data or citation demonstrating why the edge relationship does not hold or holds with opposite sign.
A quantitative test shows the opposite sign from what the edge predicts, with statistical significance.

rejected_to_speculative.description: A rejected edge may be re-proposed as speculative if new evidence contradicts the rejection reason.

requirements.description: The re-proposal must explicitly reference the original rejection record and explain why the new evidence supersedes the rejection reason.

demotion_rules.description: Rules governing backward movement in the confidence hierarchy (speculative → rejected, or flagging tested edges for re-review). Applies confidence_tier thresholds set in governance.json v1.2.

tier_1_to_3.description: Edges with confidence_tier ≤ 3 may be demoted automatically by the LLM adversarial review or by flag_edge() accumulation (≥ 3 flags, 90-day rule). No human sign-off required.

tier_4_to_5.description: Edges with confidence_tier ≥ 4 require human confirmation for demotion. The reason must be recorded in knowledge/changelog.md. Automated agents may flag these edges (setting review_required: True) but may not demote them.

seed_graph_governance.description: Rules governing changes to knowledge/seed_graph.json, which is the scientific record.
change_requirements: Data evidence or academic citation supporting the change
Quorum voting as defined in this governance.json
Corresponding entry in knowledge/changelog.md explaining what changed and why
seed_graph_governance.if_agent_believes_edge_is_wrong: Add a flagging note to knowledge/changelog.md for human review. Do NOT edit seed_graph.json.

knowledge/seed_graph.json.exception: Governed process requiring human review, data evidence, and quorum voting per this governance.json

knowledge/governance.json.exception: Project owner only; every change requires a comment in changelog.md signed by project owner

knowledge/rejected_edges.json.reason: Rejected edges form the permanent audit trail of knowledge decisions; they may be superseded but never erased

sessions/*/report_*.json.reason: Session reports are the audit trail for governance, regulatory, and board reporting purposes

entry_must_include: Evidence or source supporting the change
Human reviewer or project owner signature

what_the_system_does_not_do.description: Intentional design constraints that preserve epistemic humility and auditability. Never implement or suggest implementing the following.
prohibited: Quantitative causal effect estimation (no OLS, BIC, VAR, GMM)
Probability estimates on adverse outcomes — sign only, never magnitude
Feedback loops in causal graph traversal — DAG only, no cycles
Automated promotion of edges without citations
Any claim stronger than Rung 2 of Pearl's ladder — no counterfactuals
Deletion of any record from rejected_edges.json or session reports
